audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for divorce debate, children, rich man, camel/needle, first-last reversal, third passion prediction, servant greatness, ransom language, and Bartimaeus.

verses.restored: Rising from there, he came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan; crowds gathered again to him, and as was his custom, he taught them again.
Pharisees came and asked him whether it is lawful for a man to release his wife, testing him.
He answered, What did Moses command you?
They said, Moses permitted writing a document of release and releasing her.
Jesus said to them, Because of your hardness of heart he wrote you this command.
But from the beginning of creation, male and female he made them.
Because of this a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
and the two will become one flesh; so they are no longer two but one flesh.
Therefore what the Divine joined together, let a human not separate.
In the house the disciples questioned him again about this.
He said to them, Whoever releases his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
And if she releases her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
They were bringing children to him so that he might touch them, but the disciples rebuked them.
Seeing this, Jesus was indignant and said, Allow the children to come to me; do not hinder them, for of such is the reign of the Divine.
Amen, I say to you: whoever does not receive the reign of the Divine like a child will certainly not enter it.
Taking them in his arms, he blessed them, placing hands on them.
As he was going out on the way, one ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what shall I do so that I may inherit age-long life?
Jesus said to him, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the Divine alone.
You know the commands: Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not bear false witness, do not defraud, honor your father and mother.
He said to him, Teacher, all these I have guarded from my youth.
Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said, One thing you lack: go, sell what you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.
But he became gloomy at the word and went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, How difficult it is for those having wealth to enter the reign of the Divine.
The disciples were amazed at his words. Jesus answered again, Children, how difficult it is to enter the reign of the Divine.
It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the reign of the Divine.
They were exceedingly astonished, saying to one another, Then who is able to be saved?
Looking at them, Jesus said, With humans it is impossible, but not with the Divine; for all things are possible with the Divine.
Peter began to say to him, Look, we have left everything and followed you.
Jesus said, Amen, I say to you: there is no one who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or fields because of me and because of the good message,
who will not receive a hundredfold now in this time: houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and fields, with pressures, and in the coming age, age-long life.
But many first will be last, and the last first.

They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was going before them. They were amazed, and those following were afraid. Taking the twelve again, he began to tell them the things about to happen to him.
Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Humanity will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes; they will condemn him to death and hand him over to the nations.
They will mock him, spit on him, flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise.
James and John, sons of Zebedee, came to him, saying, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask you.
He said to them, What do you want me to do for you?
They said, Give us that we may sit, one at your right and one at your left, in your glory.
Jesus said to them, You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink or be baptized with the baptism with which I am baptized?
They said to him, We are able. Jesus said to them, The cup that I drink you will drink, and the baptism with which I am baptized you will be baptized.
But to sit at my right or left is not mine to give, except for those for whom it has been prepared.
Hearing this, the ten began to be indignant about James and John.
Calling them, Jesus said to them, You know that those seeming to rule the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
But it is not so among you. Whoever wants to become great among you will be your servant.
Whoever wants to be first among you will be slave of all.
For even the Son of Humanity did not come to be served but to serve, and to give his life/soul as a ransom for many.
They came to Jericho. As he was going out from Jericho with his disciples and a sufficient crowd, Bartimaeus son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting beside the way.
Hearing that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out and say, Son of David, Jesus, have mercy on me.
Many rebuked him so that he would be silent, but he cried out much more, Son of David, have mercy on me.
Jesus stood still and said, Call him. They called the blind man, saying, Take courage; rise, he calls you.
Throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.
Jesus answered him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Rabbouni, that I may see again.
Jesus said to him, Go; your trust has saved/healed you. Immediately he saw again and followed him on the way.
